product_summary.name: Research Server
product_summary.tagline: Claude Code remembers Monday on Friday. You manage your AI agent by email.
product_summary.description: Research Server is persistent agentic infrastructure for Claude Code power users. Sessions remember what happened last week. Agents wake up from a forwarded email, execute a task, and go back to sleep. The memory persistence recipe works in 5 minutes. Done-for-you setup available at $2,500 flat.

target_audience.primary: Claude Code power users, solo founders, Canadian tech founders with SR&ED
pain_points: Claude Code forgets everything between sessions â€” re-explaining context every time
No way to trigger Claude from an email without complex infrastructure
SR&ED diary is always behind, risks CRA rejection for post-hoc reconstruction
Want autonomous AI agents but don't want to manage infrastructure

tiers.name: Memory Persistence Recipe
features: 5 tools: memory_save, memory_read, memory_search, session_save, session_get

tiers.name: Done-For-You Setup
features: Full Research Server configured on your hardware or VPS
First month of orchestrator tasks scheduled

How Research Server solves: SR&ED diary is always behind, risks CRA rejection for post-hoc reconstruction

brand_voice.tone: Terse and technically confident — written for builders who hate fluff and recognize signal immediately. Every sentence earns its place.
do_list: Lead with concrete capability, not abstract value ('Agents wake from a forwarded email' not 'powerful automation')
Use short declarative sentences that assume technical literacy
Quantify setup friction honestly ('5 minutes', '$2,500 flat') — specificity builds trust
dont_list: Never use 'revolutionary', 'game-changing', or AI hype vocabulary
Don't explain what agents or memory are — the audience already knows
Avoid soft CTAs like 'learn more' or 'explore' — use direct action framing
